context: AF & DR Class — AF full barre to adage, port de bras, centre practice; DR full barre, port de bras, centre practice, adage, allegro 1

corrections.text: Use up all the music at the barre
Toes stay completely on the floor in tendus when returning to fifth
Weight even in plié in fourth for tendus and pliés
Rond de jambe jeté: aligned in front and behind
Timing in rond de jambe too quick
Rond en l'air: accent and resistance needs more emphasis
Don't twist shoulders in grand battements — square with hips
Move upper body over the toes in derrière
Don't contract the neck
More use of peripheral space in port de bras
Heels need to land at the same time rather than dropping into closed fifth
Think going up in the turn
Lock standing knee and pull up for more stability
Weight too far back in the heels
Lock standing knee and pull up in adage
DR: musicality is the main priority across the class
DR: projection — look beyond the arms and shoulders
DR: fourth arms — shoulders straight, not tilted to either side
Don't lift the right hip in passé / posé retiré en arrière
Don't let the working side get pulled back — hold turnout but keep shoulders and hips square
Difficult to get weight over the toes — sitting in heels even in fifth; possibly forcing turnout from the feet and gripping with heels
